title: Timing side-channel remains: key length check leaks before constant-time compare
description: webhook.rs:50-51: if expected_bytes.len() != provided_bytes.len() || expected_bytes.ct_eq(provided_bytes).unwrap_u8() != 1 — the length comparison is done first with a short-circuit ||. This leaks the length of the expected key: an attacker can determine the correct key length in O(1) by submitting keys of increasing length and observing when the short-circuit stops. For proper constant-time behavior, both length check and byte comparison must happen in constant time. The subtle crate provides ConstantTimeEq::ct_eq for slices of equal length; for unequal lengths, the correct approach is to pad/zero-extend or use a constant-time length comparison. A simpler correct fix is: use hmac::equal(expected, provided) which is length-constant, or use subtle::ConstantTimeEq on padded slices. This is a regression from the pass-1 fix that replaced == with ct_eq but kept the short-circuit length pre-check.
close_reason: Pass 3 fixes: HMAC key compare, inbox SSRF guard, domain validation, async Accept dispatch, foreign keys, IPv6 SSRF, poll timeout. Remaining P2-P3 accepted as known debt.
